description_long: This town hall is a two-story public building with a steep, dark gray hipped roof featuring four prominent dormer windows on one side. The facade is adorned with symmetrical rows of rectangular windows, each framed in brown and highlighted by decorative moldings. The main entrance is marked by a set of double arched doors, flanked by additional windows and topped with a cornice. A horizontal band separates the two floors, adding visual structure to the building. The lower facade displays a large orange sign with white lettering, indicating the building's name and function. The exterior walls are finished in a light gray color, giving the structure a formal and institutional appearance.
description: A stately two-story town hall with a steep hipped roof, dormer windows, symmetrical window rows, and a prominent sign on the facade.
description_view_0: The rear and side of the building, showing the plain light gray walls and the steep, dark gray roof without visible windows or ornamentation.
description_view_1: The front facade with three dormer windows, symmetrical window rows, and a large orange sign with white lettering above the main entrance.
description_view_2: A corner view highlighting four dormer windows, the full length of the orange sign, and the arched double doors of the main entrance.
description_view_3: The opposite side and rear, showing the roof and plain walls, with the dormer windows visible but less detail on the entrance.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Decorative cornices, window moldings, and a prominent horizontal band between floors; arched doorways and dormer window frames add visual interest.
attribute_building_signage: Large orange sign with white lettering on the lower front facade, reading 'HERMANNS & PRATZHEIM' and 'CONRAD & CO'.
attribute_entrance_design: Main entrance features double arched doors, framed by decorative moldings and topped with a cornice; no columns or portico present.
